rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded dependency addresses are used without any chain or code validation
rejected_candidates.reason: This mainly relies on wrong-network deployment or external address replacement outside the verifier's intended mainnet environment, so it is better treated as deployment/configuration risk than a reportable in-protocol vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Native-balance profit check can be spoofed by unsolicited ETH transfers
rejected_candidates.reason: An unsolicited ETH transfer increases the verifier's real balance only because the sender pays that subsidy themselves; it does not create a realistic theft, insolvency, or protocol-level loss scenario.

rejected_candidates.title: Counter state is completely unauthenticated
rejected_candidates.reason: `Counter.sol` is a standalone toy contract with no privileged role, funds, or security-sensitive integration shown in scope, so unrestricted setters are not reportable here.
